title: eriocoma nelsonii subsp. dorei
intro: Eriocoma nelsonii subsp. dorei, also known as Nelson's eriocoma, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its clusters of tiny white flowers and silvery-green foliage. Traditionally, Nelson's eriocoma has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation. The plant's health benefits include its potential to alleviate symptoms of eczema, acne, and other skin conditions, as well as its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Nelson's eriocoma is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and its essential oil can be extracted for topical application and aromatherapy use.
